item.title: cmd_backlog_edit usage message omits most supported flags
item.intent: The backlog edit help text must list all supported flags — a usage message that omits 9 of 18 flags is misleading and makes the CLI appear less capable than it is
item.description: cmd_backlog_edit() has two help text locations that list supported flags: the early usage error message (around line 3767) and the catch-all error at the end (around line 3941). The early message lists only: --title, --intent, --priority, --status, --desc, --category, --depends-on, --ready, --no-ready. It omits: --step, --clear-steps, --scope, --clear-scope, --ac, --ac-verify, --clear-ac, --groomed-at, --groomed-notes. The catch-all message at line 3941 is complete. Fix: update the early usage message to match the complete list in the catch-all.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_edit() and locate the early usage error message (around line 3767) that is shown when no ID is provided
Compare it to the complete flag list in the catch-all error message (around line 3941)
Update the early usage message to include all supported flags: --step, --clear-steps, --scope, --clear-scope, --ac, --ac-verify, --clear-ac, --groomed-at, --groomed-notes
Verify that 'backlog edit' with no arguments shows the updated usage message
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'backlog edit' with no arguments shows a usage message that includes --step, --clear-steps, --ac, --ac-verify, --clear-ac, --scope, --clear-scope, --groomed-at, --groomed-notes
The complete flag list in the catch-all error message is unchanged
No functional behavior changes — this is display only
